sanskrit:
अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम्।
यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम्॥
transliteration:
avyakto 'kṣara ity uktas tam āhuḥ paramāṃ gatim
yaṃ prāpya na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṃ mama
literal_meaning: Unmanifest, imperishable—thus is declared that supreme goal; reaching which they return not—that is My supreme abode.
contextual_meaning: Krishna points to a psychological ‘home base’ beyond every changing mood, role, or trauma narrative: the unchanging awareness that witnesses yet never itself becomes the story. Identifying with this ‘imperishable’ observer ends the exhausting cycle of re-traumatization and compulsive repetition.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth; shift from egoic self to witnessing awareness; cessation of rumination and behavioral relapse.
trigger_scenario: After years of relapse into addiction or toxic relationships, a client realizes the ‘story’ is not who they are; they begin to abide as the non-changing awareness behind the story, finally breaking the loop.
anchor_text: Rest as the changeless witness behind every thought, feeling, and story.
